item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the humour in the SCP. +1
I had to chuckle, trying to imagine what would happen should he attempt "Through Fire and Flames" by Dragonforce. +1
Finding out the idea was concepted from Cacofonix the bard? All my yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to upvote, but with an entry like "Here Comes the Sun", I want to downvote because of inept it makes the rest of the article. Can't that be deleted? I would upvote it gladly otherwise.

EDIT: Or at least have it there, but the guards tranquilizing SCP-2143 before it keeps playing. Just anything besides a [DATA EXPUNGED].

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Regurgitated Guts
vaginal lubrication

oh god my sides
I cant stop laughing

take all my upboats

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Numerous egg custard pies were produced. The custard of each pie contained vaginal lubrication.

Ewwwwwww.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All instances appear to have been killed by the fall or being crushed by other Luther Vandrosses

Heh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't help feeling the way Apollo is portrayed is a little boring. I know that he already has an established personality, but the way he talks an acts sounds like he's from that Olympians book series by Rick Riordan. The concept is fun, but the character isn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two (2) guards equipped with tasers are to be stationed outside SCP-2143's containment chamber at all times.

If the Foundation selected guards who can't recognize one of the most popular songs by one of the most well-known bands in history, then the Foundation is irredeemably inept and the world is thoroughly screwed. I realize that a plague spreads if he doesn't play, but for crying out loud, if you hear Here Comes the Freaking Sun, you taze the guy, stop the XK, and put up with the plague for a day.

Edit: This goes for the guys in charge of Kolokol-2 as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Based on Researcher Dios and PeppersGhost's comments, it looks like there might be other people who are unclear on the nature of which songs SCP-2143 plays.

Basically, the Foundation has almost NO control over what songs SCP-2143 performs. SCP-2143 randomly picks and performs a song of its own (lack of) volition. They can ask it to replay an already-performed song once more, but that's it.

I will clarify that in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I didn't care for that either. I think I wanted to like it, but it was just too goofy overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept, but the writing (as in, the tone and flow in places) and some of the details in the story make me sort of just neutral on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't see how this is different from the version that got deleted a while back. Everything looks essentially the same, including the complete insanity of the Foundation allowing it to play Here Comes the Sun, which is why I downvoted in the first place. Seriously, there's no way that could end well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually would have guessed Dionysus, but I'm not that good with Greek mythology. Anyway, i'd say it's good enough for an upvote, but not spectacular.

Also, trying to picture a lyre cover of "regurgitated guts" makes my head hurt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The character in the piece is Apollo.

The inspiration for the piece is a character from a Franco-Belgian comic called "Asterix" whose singing is so bad that it literally causes it to rain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fun stuff. This could use a collaboration addendum for more creative entries.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hope someone figures it out soon, because I'm neutral voting right now and I hope that the big reveal can change that.

I'm not sure what it is about this one that left me feeling sorta meh about it. Like, the idea of it raining objects related to the song being played seems original, but overall it just felt like it was just an excuse to have a wacky interview and list of goofy anomalous events. Maybe I'm just burned out on that sort of thing, I dunno.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really? I was thinking his true identity was Apollo, since he called talking his father like "talking to lightning", and how he mentioned the guy's untethered sex drive. And the whole music shebang.

Regardless… I like this. It's a good take on the humanoid SCP genre, the likes of which have been painfully sparse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After one failed post a couple months ago, Singin' in the Rain returns with a fresh coat of paint!

Thanks to everyone in chat over the last four months for helping me get this off the ground, especially Athena_Grey with her bottomless passion for Greek mythology.

Props to whoever can figure out the true inspiration for this piece(hint: it's not Greek)!

See more of my work on my author page!
